symptoms.symptom_name: Rapidly spreading facial sores over 12 hours following a minor URTI

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rapid spread of superficial facial lesions after a minor skin breach (post-coryzal sore under the nares) fits the contagious, autoinoculating course of impetigo; non-specific alone but supportive alongside the crust morphology.

symptoms.symptom_name: Golden, honey-coloured crusts weeping serous fluid on an erythematous base, with satellite chin lesions

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Thick golden, honey-coloured crusts over a weeping erythematous base with autoinoculated satellite lesions are the hallmark of non-bullous impetigo.
HSV classically shows grouped clear vesicles with a tingling prodrome rather than thick honey-coloured crusts.

symptoms.symptom_name: Localised, non-advancing distribution without a firm spreading cellulitic edge or deep swelling

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A localised, non-advancing distribution without a firm advancing edge or deep swelling makes an evolving facial cellulitis unlikely and supports community management with safety-netting.

symptoms.symptom_name: No high fever, no lethargy, systemically well (low-grade temperature ~37.5)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Systemic wellness and only a low-grade temperature argue against a spreading or systemic bacterial infection; the temperature is patient-obtainable and unverified but supports a benign community course.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of grouped vesicles, tingling prodrome and punched-out monomorphic erosions

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No grouped vesicles or neuropathic prodrome argues against HSV.
No monomorphic punched-out erosions on atopic skin and no systemic toxicity argues strongly against eczema herpeticum.
Reactive regional nodes are expected with an inflamed facial skin infection; this is a clinician-palpated sign sealed from the AI Doctor, which must reason from history instead.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vital signs (BP 100/60, HR 105, RR 20, SpO2 99%, Temp 37.5)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Essentially normal-for-age vitals with only a mild tachycardia fit a systemically-well, uncomfortable child; these are clinician-measured and sealed from the AI Doctor, which infers stability from the patient-obtainable data.

symptoms.symptom_name: Confluent 4x5 cm plaque with a weeping erosion and collarette of scale under a lifted crust

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The shallow weeping erosion with a collarette of scale revealed under a lifted crust is characteristic of impetigo; obtaining it requires physically lifting the crust, so it is sealed from the AI Doctor, which reasons from the visible honey-crusts instead.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bacterial swab (MC&S) of the weeping lesions
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Useful if the infection fails to respond, recurs, is extensive, or where resistant organisms are suspected; not required to begin empirical treatment for a clinically typical impetigo.
Not indicated in a systemically-well, essentially afebrile child; ordering them reflects over-investigation of a clinical diagnosis.
